This development comes after the game was previously rated for a younger audience by the Entertainment Software Rating Board (ESRB).
summary::Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 s: Final Fantasy 7 Remake Part 3 is reportedly set to appear at Summer Game Fest according to a leaker behind Star Fox and Nintendo Switch 2 tip-offs. This development comes as the game's release date has been delayed multiple times, with some sources suggesting it could be released in the near future. The remake's production team has been tight-lipped about its progress, fueling speculation among fans.
summary::The Odyssey \"largest screen\": The Odyssey "largest screen" IMAX tickets are almost on sale and can be purchased online, with availability starting from a specific date. This development is significant for developers who may want to create immersive gaming experiences or use the technology in their own projects. The tickets offer an unparalleled cinematic experience, allowing users to watch movies in IMAX format.
summary::Hopes for a Star Wars horror mo: The director of Deadpool and Wolverine, Shawn Levy, has an idea for a Star Wars horror movie mashup. He envisions a film that combines the dark humor and action of both franchises with the sci-fi elements of Star Wars. The potential project would allow for unique world-building and character interactions between characters from different universes.
summary::\"The timing is impossible to i: Ubisoft attended an invitation-only meeting with the EU Commission ahead of responding to The Crew shutdown, but was not invited. This timing is impossible to ignore as it suggests a lack of transparency in the company's handling of the game's issues. The move has sparked a campaign by Stop Killing Games, which argues that Ubisoft's actions are part of a broader pattern of corporate malfeasance.
summary::Star Wars Zero Company release: Star Wars Zero Company release date leaks one day before Summer Game Fest, as XCOM veterans' new strategy game joins the crowd avoiding GTA 6. The game's developer has confirmed that it will be released on July 12th, just a day before the annual Summer Game Fest event. This move is seen as a strategic decision to capitalize on the gaming community's excitement for the festival.
summary::Nintendo announces new Switch 2: Nintendo has announced a new Switch model with a replaceable battery, designed to meet European Union regulations for waste reduction. The company plans to discontinue the existing battery after a certain period of use, replacing it with a new one. This move is expected to reduce electronic waste and minimize environmental impact.
summary::Production of DDR4 memory and m: Production of DDR4 memory and motherboards is restarting amid unprecedented memory shortages, with the PC industry preparing for a world without DDR5. This shift in focus reflects the increasing demand for high-speed storage solutions as companies scramble to meet growing consumer needs. The shortage has significant implications for manufacturers and consumers alike, highlighting the importance of efficient supply chain management.
summary::Hyte shows off Y50 chassis, aes: Hyte has released the Y50 chassis, a sub-$100 laptop that brings value to its Y-series lineup by offering new fans and an aesthetic cable accessory kit. The company also updated Nexus Software, now available as a web-based platform, allowing users to access their files from any device. Additionally, Hyte introduced new fans, which provide improved cooling performance. These updates demonstrate the brand's commitment to delivering high-performance laptops at affordable prices.
summary::I Hope Kratos Doesn’t Show Up A: God of War Laufey's Kratos cameo raises concerns about his involvement in the game's story. The character's brief appearance worries the author that it may undermine Faye's development and the overall narrative. The author believes that having Kratos around would detract from the project's focus on exploring Faye's life beyond her husband's influence. They also want to see a more nuanced portrayal of the characters' relationships, particularly between Faye and her family members.
summary::Man of Tomorrow leak suggests B: Man of Tomorrow leak suggests Brainiac has Lex Luthor in his sights, not Superman. The leaked information implies that Brainiac's plans may be more focused on Lex Luthor than the Man of Steel, potentially altering the DC Universe's dynamics. This development could have significant implications for the storylines and characters within the DC Comics universe.
summary::Subnautica 2's first major upda: Subnautica 2's first major update brings back one of the most requested player experiences and promises to be the scariest the game has been yet, according to GamesRadar+.
summary::Helldivers 2 promises \"the nex: Helldivers 2 promises an increased level cap, new missions and more in its huge progression overhaul. The game's developer, Devolver Digital, is also introducing a new "Major Orders" system which will provide players with a sense of progression and direction. This change is expected to have a significant impact on the game's overall design and player experience.
summary::Paralives proves it's a true Si: Paralives has proven itself to be a true successor to the Sims 4, with developers desperately trying to fix a bug where a naked man can appear in your household if you exit without saving. The issue is related to the game's save system and requires a patch to be applied to resolve the problem.
summary::Mina The Hollower&#8216;s Weird: Mina The Hollower's robust set of modifiers allows players to change how the game plays, from making enemies attack first to having floatier jumps. This feature is especially fun when combined with the game's already challenging platforming, and players can create a wide range of bizarre effects by turning on or off various modifiers.
summary::Backrooms director says Valve i: Valve's Backrooms is an inspiration for game developers due to its unique blend of atmospheric sound design and eerie visuals, which can be used as a starting point to create immersive experiences. By incorporating meaningful motivations behind the game's core concept, developers can elevate their own projects and attract a dedicated audience.
summary::The Odyssey trailer can now be: The Odyssey trailer can now be watched in every aspect ratio that it's available in, and the difference with IMAX 70mm is incredible. The change allows for a more cinematic experience as the trailer can be viewed at its original width or any other aspect ratio without stretching or cropping. This improvement is likely to enhance the overall viewing experience of gamers.
summary::Steam Machine is coming this su: Steam Machine is coming this summer, Valve confirms, but its lack of pricing has me raising an eyebrow as hardware prices surge. The company's decision to delay the release of the Steam Machine until later in the year may be a sign that they are prioritizing other factors over meeting consumer demand for the device. As hardware prices continue to rise, it remains to be seen whether Valve will be able to deliver on its promise of a affordable and feature-rich gaming PC.
summary::Steam just got a huge new \"ref: Steam's storefront has undergone a significant refresh, moving from its top-to-bottom design to incorporating modern features and improvements. The update includes new visual elements, improved user interface, and enhanced functionality, making it more appealing to developers who create games and apps for the platform.
summary::Crimson Desert continues its qu: Crimson Desert continues its quest to include every feature ever as it adds Pinball, baby chicks that grow into mounts, and new re-blockade improvements. The game's developers are committed to making the game more immersive by incorporating these features, which will enhance gameplay and provide players with a unique experience.
summary::Outlook may have allowed unencr: Outlook may have allowed unencrypted connections for decades, report claims. A recent upgrade to Fedora and Dovecot reveals a protocol downgrade issue present since at least 2007. This means that users of these systems are vulnerable to eavesdropping and tampering with encrypted data. The discovery highlights the need for developers to prioritize encryption in their applications.
